title: sedum hirsutum subsp. euhirsutum
intro: Sedum hirsutum subsp. euhirsutum, also known as the hairy stonecrop, is a subspecies of succulent plant belonging to the genus Sedum, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Sedum hirsutum subsp. euhirsutum include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating wounds and burns. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves to utilize its medicinal properties, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
